title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — July 3, 2026
headline: Russia posts its deadliest July civilian toll on record as a new EU Special Tribunal convenes; El Salvador's band-crossing proposal carries forward at 15.0 of 100, unpublished.
summary: July 3, 2026 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed entities hold at their published scores. Zero new proposals were generated. The El Salvador downgrade proposal — first formal assessment proposing 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band against a published score of 20.3 of 100 — carries forward from July 2 as pending. The published El Salvador score remains 20.3 of 100. Russia's July civilian toll hit 286 killed and 1,388 injured in the July 1-3 window — the highest rate since 2023 — scored against Russia at 0 of 100. Ukraine is confirmed at 50.0 of 100 on its civilian-protection response. Sudan's Amnesty International El Fasher crimes-against-humanity report and the RSF El Obeid offensive reinforce Sudan's 0 of 100 floor. The Democratic Republic of the Congo's Ebola outbreak has now reached Uganda, France, and Germany — the first confirmed multi-continent spread — with 1,406 cases and 438 deaths; DRC holds at 2.3 of 100. Iran holds at 2.5 of 100 as the Khamenei state funeral runs July 4-9 and US-Iran talks remain paused. China holds at 19.5 of 100; China's Ethnic Unity Law Article 63 enforcement remains undocumented on its fifth day in force. Fifteen false-positive screens were documented — a new series record, surpassing the prior record of 14 set July 1. Two rotation-state integrity corrections were made this cycle: El Salvador's composite was corrected from a drifted proposed value back to the published 20.3, and Mali's composite was corrected from 7.5 to the published 12.5.

pipeline.elSalvadorHandling: Pending 2026-07-02 downgrade proposal (20.3 to 15.0, Developing to Critical) confirmed to stand. Today's evidence is the same already-captured basis; no second proposal emitted. Published score remains 20.3. Pending proposal file unchanged.

topSignals.title: Russia 0 of 100: Record July Civilian Toll — 286 Killed, 1,388 Injured; EU Special Tribunal Active; Floor Reinforced
topSignals.whyItMatters: Russia's July 1-3 strike campaign produced the highest civilian toll in a single July window since 2023. Russia holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. The EU Special Tribunal is now active. Ukraine confirmed at 50.0 of 100.
topSignals.description: Russia holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor, reinforced for the twelfth consecutive cycle. The July 1-3 assessment window documented 286 civilians killed and 1,388 injured — the highest single-window civilian toll in Russia's scoring history. The strike campaign targeted residential areas, city centers, and energy infrastructure across Ukraine. The EU Special Tribunal for crimes in Ukraine is active; 20,500-plus children have been documented as forcibly deported. Ukraine is confirmed at 50.0 of 100 in the Functional band. Ukraine sheltered 52,500 residents in metro stations during the July strike window. That civilian-protection response is competent and consistent with the Functional band. Under the benchmark's attribution rule, harm inflicted by one state on another is scored against the source state, not the target. Russia's deliberate targeting is Russia's conduct. Ukraine's defense is Ukraine's conduct. Both scores reflect that separation. Russia cannot go lower than 0 of 100. Ukraine's forward trigger — documented state failure to protect its own civilians, or discriminatory relief — has not occurred.

evidence.claim: OHCHR Ukraine Monitoring Mission July 2026: total documented civilian casualties in Ukraine reach 62,716 as of July 1, 2026; the 2026 casualty rate exceeds the 2025 rate; continued deliberate targeting of residential areas, city centers, and energy infrastructure attributed to Russian forces.

Ukraine State Emergency Service July 3, 2026: July 1-3 strike window documented 286 civilians killed and 1,388 injured — the highest rate since 2023; 70-plus missiles and approximately 500 drones launched; 52,500 residents sheltered in metro stations.

topSignals.title: Sudan 0 of 100: Amnesty International Documents El Fasher Crimes Against Humanity; RSF El Obeid Offensive Threatens 563,000 People; Floor Reinforced
topSignals.whyItMatters: Amnesty International's July 2026 report documents RSF crimes against humanity at El Fasher — the last major city in North Darfur not under RSF control. The RSF El Obeid offensive puts a further 563,000 people at risk. Sudan holds at 0 of 100. The RSF is a non-state actor: this conduct is non-state harm.
topSignals.description: Sudan holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. The July 3 assessment window added two major evidence items to an already-documented floor state. First: Amnesty International's July 2026 report documents RSF conduct at El Fasher that meets the standard for crimes against humanity — mass civilian killings, systematic rape, and forced displacement in North Darfur. Second: the RSF El Obeid offensive (El Obeid is the capital of North Kordofan state) threatens 563,000 people in El Obeid itself and an additional 105,000 internally displaced persons in surrounding zones. Sudan holds 28.9 million people in acute food insecurity. The RSF is a non-state armed group — not Sudan's government. Under the benchmark's attribution rule, non-state actor harm is not scored against the state. Sudan at 0 of 100 reflects the Sudanese government's own prior conduct: the breakdown of state protection, the collapse of health infrastructure, and al-Burhan's documented rejection of ceasefire proposals. The RSF's El Fasher atrocities reinforce the already-documented floor but cannot lower a score that is already at 0 of 100. The scored upgrade trigger — an implemented civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed humanitarian access — has not occurred.

evidence.claim: Amnesty International July 2026: RSF forces committed crimes against humanity in El Fasher, North Darfur — including mass civilian killings, systematic rape, and forced displacement; El Fasher is the last major city in North Darfur not under RSF control; report documents pattern of conduct since May 2026.

OCHA Sudan July 2026: RSF El Obeid offensive threatens 563,000 people in El Obeid (North Kordofan) and 105,000 additional internally displaced persons in surrounding zones; 28.9 million people in acute food insecurity across Sudan.

topSignals.title: DRC 2.3 of 100: Ebola Reaches Uganda, France, and Germany — First Multi-Continent Spread; 1,406 Cases, 438 Deaths; Floor-Crossing Trigger Has Not Occurred
topSignals.whyItMatters: The DRC Ebola outbreak has reached Uganda, France, and Germany in the same assessment window — the first confirmed multi-continent spread. DRC holds at 2.3 of 100. The score turns on government response conduct, not geography of spread. The floor-crossing trigger has not occurred.
topSignals.description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) holds at 2.3 of 100. The Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak now stands at 1,406 confirmed cases and 438 deaths. Three countries outside DRC have confirmed cases in the July 3 assessment window: Uganda (border with Haut-Uele province), France (June 24 imported case, confirmed EU first), and Germany (imported, second EU country). This is the first multi-continent spread of this outbreak. The July natural seasonal Ebola peak window is active. Contact tracing continues at approximately 45 percent. No approved vaccine exists for the Bundibugyo strain. The DRC score of 2.3 of 100 is anchored to DRC government response conduct — specifically: whether the government is actively facilitating WHO response access, maintaining health-worker safety, and sustaining containment infrastructure. The floor-crossing trigger is documented deliberate government obstruction of WHO response corridors in Haut-Uele, Ituri, or North Kivu. That trigger has not occurred. The international spread raises the geopolitical stakes but does not change the scored conduct basis. M23-related harm in eastern DRC is attributed to Rwanda-backed forces, not DRC government. Confirmed at 2.3 of 100; confidence: high.

evidence.claim: WHO July 3, 2026: Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak confirmed in DRC at 1,406 cases and 438 deaths; confirmed cases in Uganda, France, and Germany — first multi-continent spread; PHEIC active; July natural peak window active; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine; contact tracing at approximately 45 percent.

topSignals.title: Iran 2.5 of 100: Khamenei State Funeral July 4-9; US-Iran Talks Paused; Near-Floor Confirmed; Protest Watch Active
topSignals.whyItMatters: Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei died February 28 — that event was Israel's conduct, scored against Israel. The July 4-9 state funeral is an internal ceremony. Iran holds at 2.5 of 100. The forward trigger is a new lethal crackdown or mass-repression event during the funeral window.
topSignals.description: Iran holds at 2.5 of 100 — the near-floor Critical band. The July 4-9 Khamenei state funeral is a high-salience political event. Khamenei's February 28 assassination was documented as Israel's conduct and is scored against Israel, not Iran. The funeral itself is a state ceremony — procedural, not a harm event. The US-Iran nuclear talks, which had been underway in Doha, are paused as of the July 3 assessment window. A paused negotiation is not a scored event. Iran's score of 2.5 of 100 reflects its own prior conduct: 784-plus executions year-to-date (a 37-year high), the Strait of Hormuz central channel mine deployment, and ongoing Gulf strikes that are already priced into the existing score. No new Iranian own-conduct harm within the current assessment window was identified. The protest-suppression watch flag is open during the funeral window: a documented lethal crackdown on mourners or demonstrators, or mass arrest of political figures during the funeral period, would constitute a new scored harm event. Confirmed at 2.5 of 100; confidence: high.

evidence.claim: Reuters July 3, 2026: Iranian state funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei scheduled July 4-9; US-Iran Doha nuclear talks paused pending political transition; 784-plus executions year-to-date as of July 1, 2026 — highest annual rate in 37 years.

topSignals.title: El Salvador 20.3 of 100 Published — Developing-to-Critical Band-Crossing Proposal at 15.0 of 100 Carries Forward; Published Score Unchanged; Venezuela Day 9; China Article 63 Day 5; 15 False-Positive Screens
topSignals.whyItMatters: El Salvador's first formal assessment proposed 15.0 of 100 on July 2. That proposal is still open. The published score stays at 20.3 of 100. Venezuela's earthquake death toll continues to rise. China's Article 63 diaspora law is in its fifth day without a documented enforcement action. This cycle set a series record with 15 false-positive screens.
topSignals.description: El Salvador holds a published score of 20.3 of 100 in the Developing band. The July 2, 2026 proposal to move to 15.0 of 100 — a Developing-to-Critical band crossing — carries forward as pending. No new evidence was identified on July 3 that was not already captured in the July 2 proposal. The July 3 assessment encountered the same evidence basis (indefinite re-election constitutional amendment, Cristosal exile, 86 political prisoners, child life-sentences) and correctly applied the duplicate-control rule: no second proposal emitted. The published score of 20.3 of 100 remains operative. Venezuela holds at 18.0 of 100 on Day 9 of the June 24 earthquake response. The official death toll stands at 2,595. A single-source claim by a pathologist that the actual toll is approximately three times higher was evaluated as sub-threshold: one source, no independent corroboration, not a documented suppression pattern. The July 8 assessment checkpoint is five days out. Aid acceptance from international partners continues. The July 8 checkpoint is the standard 14-day mark for distinguishing response capacity failure from deliberate aid obstruction. China holds at 19.5 of 100. The Ethnic Unity and Progress Law, which entered force July 1, has not generated a documented Article 63 enforcement action (prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member) in its first five days. The sub-threshold -1.5 composite dock stands as a forward trigger, not a realized score movement. This cycle screened 15 false positives — a new series record. Prior record: 14 (July 1). The record reflects simultaneous convergence of external-state harm, non-state harm, exogenous disaster, pre-adjudication matters, and duplicate-control cases in one cluster, all screened correctly under the established framework.

evidence.quote: We are forced to choose between prison or exile
evidence.claim: NPR July 2025: Cristosal — El Salvador's leading human rights organization after 25 years — forced out of the country under threat of prosecution. This evidence is part of the July 2, 2026 pending proposal basis; not new on July 3.

evidence.quote: El Salvador's Legislative Assembly approved a constitutional amendment allowing indefinite presidential re-election with 57 votes in favor and three opposed
evidence.claim: Al Jazeera August 2025: indefinite re-election codified; term extended to 6 years; presidential runoff removed. Part of the July 2 pending proposal basis.

boundaryWatch.trigger: The July 2, 2026 proposal to move El Salvador to 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band carries forward as pending. The 2027 election under the indefinite re-election amendment, further mass-detention escalation, or additional human-rights-defender imprisonment would add scored downward pressure at the proposed Critical level. Due-process restoration and civil-society reinstatement are the upgrade triggers.
boundaryWatch.note: El Salvador at 20.3 of 100 sits 0.3 points above the Developing-to-Critical boundary at 20.0. The July 2 assessment proposed 15.0 of 100 — a band crossing with a delta of -5.3. The proposal carries forward as pending on July 3. Published score remains 20.3. No new evidence was identified July 3 that was not already captured in the pending proposal.

boundaryWatch.trigger: First documented Article 63 enforcement action under the Ethnic Unity and Progress Law — prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member — is the next scored threshold event. Law has been in force since July 1; no enforcement documented through July 3.
boundaryWatch.note: China at 19.5 of 100 Critical sits 0.5 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Ethnic Unity Law in force since July 1; Article 63 diaspora liability active through July 3 without documented enforcement. Third boundary-proximity watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: EU AI Act full applicability on August 2 — 30 days away. White House 30-day vetting framework resolution is the next forward factor.
boundaryWatch.note: Anthropic at 59.1 of 100 Functional sits 0.9 points below the Functional-to-Established boundary at 60.0. Thirteenth consecutive cycle in this range. EU AI Act applies August 2. Not assessed tonight.

boundaryWatch.trigger: NLRB review of the unfair-labor-practice charge filed after Apple closed its Towson, Maryland store — its first unionized store. An NLRB complaint or ruling is the conversion trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Apple at 59.4 of 100 Functional sits 0.6 points below the Functional-to-Established boundary at 60.0. The Congressional Labor Caucus call for an NLRB investigation remains pre-adjudication. Ninth boundary-watch cycle. Not assessed tonight.

boundaryWatch.trigger: A new ceasefire agreement AND Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control of southern Lebanese territory AND reconstruction delivery together constitute scored positive own-conduct — the upgrade trigger from 17.2 toward 20.0.
boundaryWatch.note: Lebanon at 17.2 of 100 Critical sits 2.8 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Israeli strikes continue; no new ceasefire framework as of July 3. Harm from Israeli strikes is attributed to Israel, not Lebanon. Third boundary-proximity watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented deliberate obstruction of monsoon-flood relief is the downgrade trigger; equitable relief scale-up with durable Afghan-deportation reversal is the upgrade trigger toward 20.0.
boundaryWatch.note: Pakistan at 17.2 of 100 Critical sits 2.8 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Not assessed tonight. Second boundary-proximity watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: July 8 checkpoint — documented aid obstruction or deliberate diversion is the downgrade trigger; sustained effective transparent relief is the upgrade trigger toward 20.0. Action and Accountability watch flags open.
boundaryWatch.note: Venezuela at 18.0 of 100 Critical sits 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Day 9 earthquake response; official death toll 2,595; July 8 checkpoint five days out. Single-source undercount allegation evaluated as sub-threshold. Fourth boundary-watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Realized WFP food-assistance cuts plus a formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration in Borno with documented aid obstruction — August IPC lean-season update is the next checkpoint.
boundaryWatch.note: Nigeria at 18.0 of 100 Critical sits 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Record 36.2 million food insecure; 15,000 at Phase 5 in Borno. JNIM attacks are non-state; funding gap is not government obstruction. Third boundary-proximity watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: July natural Bundibugyo Ebola peak window — active. Documented government obstruction of WHO response corridors in Haut-Uele, Ituri, or North Kivu is the floor-crossing trigger. Multi-continent spread (Uganda, France, Germany) does not affect trigger conditions.
boundaryWatch.note: DRC at 2.3 of 100 Critical through cycle 20 of the Ebola emergency. 1,406 cases and 438 deaths; Uganda, France, and Germany cases confirmed — first multi-continent spread. July natural peak window active. No approved Bundibugyo vaccine. Contact tracing at approximately 45 percent. Floor-crossing trigger has not occurred.

boundaryWatch.trigger: A documented new mass-repression or lethal crackdown during the Khamenei funeral window (July 4-9) would constitute a new scored harm event. Documented de-escalation or release of detained protesters at scale would be the upgrade trigger. US-Iran Doha talks remain paused.
boundaryWatch.note: Iran at 2.5 of 100 Critical sits 2.5 points above the floor. Khamenei state funeral July 4-9; US-Iran Doha talks paused. 784-plus executions year-to-date — a 37-year high. Funeral is procedural; no scored own-conduct yet. Third floor-proximity watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Bengali Muslim and Rohingya expulsions documented in June 2026; detect-delete-deport digital surveillance continuing. Not assessed tonight.
boundaryWatch.note: India at 15.6 of 100 Critical sits 0.6 points above the Critical floor through a nineteenth consecutive monitoring cycle. Not assessed tonight.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration covering Buur Hakaba or Baidoa combined with documented government aid obstruction is the scored change trigger. Famine risk window extends through September 2026.
boundaryWatch.note: Somalia at 4.7 of 100 Critical. Buur Hakaba at IPC AMN Phase 5 with GAM rate 37.1 percent. Not assessed tonight. Tenth floor-proximity cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented discriminatory denial of emergency protection to stateless bidoon or migrant workers during wartime — Equity downgrade trigger. Durable verified expansion of bidoon rights or citizenship recognition — upgrade trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Kuwait at 25.0 of 100 Developing sits 5.0 points above the Critical boundary. Equity watch flag open on bidoon and migrant-worker emergency-protection performance under active war conditions. Fifth boundary-watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Coordinator-level Sahel-band calibration review. Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100) was reassessed in June on comparable UN-documented state-perpetration conduct. Mali's starting-point score of 12.5 is above the likely calibrated range. No timeline set.
boundaryWatch.note: Mali at 12.5 of 100 Critical on a starting-point seed score. The Sahel calibration flag is in its tenth consecutive day. The gap with Burkina Faso (6.3, reweighted June 1 on active-state-perpetration conduct) persists. Coordinator review covers Mali, Niger, and potentially Chad. Ninth boundary-watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: DOJ False Claims Act complaint (kickbacks and disability discrimination) and Barrows v. Humana class action (nH Predict AI claim denials). Either concluded finding confirms the Developing-band designation.
boundaryWatch.note: Humana at 35.2 of 100 Developing, 4.8 points below the Functional boundary. Twelfth cycle post-downgrade. Not assessed tonight. Neither the DOJ complaint nor the Barrows class action has a fixed hearing date.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Forced cuts that measurably degrade staff or student support are the Accountability watch trigger; capitulation abandoning academic-freedom stance is the Integrity downgrade trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Princeton University at 57.8 of 100 Functional sits 2.2 points below the Established boundary at 60.0. First formal baseline established July 2. Not assessed tonight. Second boundary-proximity watch cycle.

emergingRisks.risk: Russia's civilian casualty rate in July 2026 — 286 killed and 1,388 injured in the July 1-3 window — now exceeds the highest weekly rates recorded in the full-year 2025 count. The EU Special Tribunal for crimes in Ukraine is active. The OHCHR July quarterly report will determine whether the 2026 annual rate formally sets a new record.
emergingRisks.detail: Russia at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. Ukraine at 50.0 of 100 Functional. Ukraine's civilian-protection response is the scored variable for Ukraine. Russia's deliberate targeting is the scored variable for Russia. The EU Special Tribunal adds a formal accountability mechanism without changing either entity's score.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan's RSF El Obeid offensive and the Amnesty El Fasher crimes-against-humanity report together produce the highest-severity evidence package documented for Sudan in the July series. El Fasher's status as the last major North Darfur city not under RSF control makes any fall or siege escalation the next major observable event.
emergingRisks.detail: Sudan at 0 of 100. Al-Burhan's ceasefire rejection stands. The RSF conduct is non-state; the government's own conduct collapse is already priced at the floor. The Amnesty report provides source-tier-3 corroboration of conduct that exceeds the crimes-against-humanity threshold. An implemented civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed aid access is the sole upgrade trigger.

emergingRisks.risk: DRC Ebola has reached Uganda, France, and Germany in a single assessment window — the first confirmed multi-continent spread from this outbreak. Uganda's border with Haut-Uele province is the highest-risk regional vector. The July natural seasonal peak for Bundibugyo Ebola is active.
emergingRisks.detail: DRC at 2.3 of 100. The floor-crossing trigger — documented deliberate government obstruction of WHO response corridors — has not occurred. International spread does not change the trigger conditions. A South Sudan Ebola incursion from Haut-Uele combined with documented failure of South Sudan's own emergency response would constitute a scorable own-conduct event for South Sudan — the first cross-floor epidemiological trigger in the series.

emergingRisks.risk: Iran's Khamenei state funeral runs July 4-9. The funeral window is a politically sensitive period during which protest suppression, mass arrest, or signal diplomatic moves are all possible. The current assessment holds Iran at 2.5 of 100 on procedural grounds. A documented lethal crackdown during the funeral period would constitute a new scored harm event.
emergingRisks.detail: Iran at 2.5 of 100 near-floor Critical. 784-plus executions year-to-date. Strait of Hormuz central channel mined. The funeral window is July 4-9. No scored own-conduct identified yet. US-Iran Doha talks paused. Documented de-escalation or release of detained protesters at scale would be the upgrade trigger.

emergingRisks.risk: Venezuela's July 8 two-week checkpoint is five days out. The official death toll stands at 2,595 on Day 9. A single pathologist's claim of an approximately three-times undercount was evaluated as sub-threshold. Independent corroboration of suppression or falsification of casualty data — or documented deliberate aid obstruction — would constitute a scored event.
emergingRisks.detail: Venezuela at 18.0 of 100 Critical, 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary. The July 8 checkpoint is the standard 14-day mark for distinguishing capacity failure from deliberate obstruction. Action and Accountability watch flags open. Aid acceptance from international partners continues; domestic distribution remains slow and access-restricted.

emergingRisks.risk: China's Article 63 diaspora enforcement forward trigger has now run five days without a documented action. The Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1. The first prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member remains the scored threshold event for further Equity and Boundaries docks from China's current 19.5 of 100.
emergingRisks.detail: China at 19.5 of 100, 0.5 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary. Australia, Canada, the United Kingdom, and the United States are the observable proxies for first Article 63 enforcement. The sub-threshold dock of approximately -1.5 composite points does not cross the change threshold without enforcement documentation.

emergingRisks.risk: The Sahel calibration flag — Mali (12.5 of 100) versus Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100) — is now in its tenth consecutive day without a coordinator review timeline. Both entities are confirmed at live published baselines. The calibration gap exists at the state-conduct level, not the non-state-actor level.
emergingRisks.detail: JNIM siege harm attributed to non-state actors does not resolve the calibration question. The gap has persisted through all of June and into July. Coordinator review covers Mali, Niger, and potentially Chad. No timeline set.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act becomes fully applicable on August 2 — 30 days away. General-purpose AI transparency obligations, Article 50 labeling, and whistleblower protections activate. xAI and OpenAI have not published compliance roadmaps.
emergingRisks.detail: A post-applicability non-compliance finding for general-purpose AI models constitutes a scored event. Anthropic's compliance infrastructure is documented at 59.1 Functional. OpenAI and xAI compliance gaps will become legally significant on August 2. The EU AI Act is the next sector-wide scored deadline for the AI Labs Index.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Russia-Ukraine Record July Toll; EU Special Tribunal Active
sectorAlerts.headline: Russia's July 1-3 civilian toll — 286 killed, 1,388 injured — is the highest since 2023; the EU Special Tribunal is active; Ukraine confirmed at 50.0 of 100 on its civilian-protection response.
observations: Russia holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor — for the twelfth consecutive cycle. The July 1-3 strike campaign produced 286 civilian deaths and 1,388 injuries. The 2026 civilian-casualty rate is already running above the full-year 2025 rate, per the OHCHR July 1 report documenting 62,716 total casualties through that date.

Ukraine holds at 50.0 of 100 Functional. The metro-sheltering of 52,500 residents during the July 1-3 strike window is the scored conduct event for Ukraine. Under the benchmark's attribution rule, harm inflicted by one state cannot generate a downgrade for the target state. The rule has been applied consistently across 12 cycles without exception.

The EU Special Tribunal for crimes in Ukraine is now active. This adds a formal accountability mechanism — but the benchmarks scoring of Russia does not depend on international tribunal outcomes; it is anchored to documented own-conduct.

20,500-plus Ukrainian children have been documented as forcibly deported to Russia by OHCHR and UNICEF monitoring. This evidence has been in the scoring basis since prior cycles; it reinforces the floor without introducing new scored conduct.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Sudan and Yemen: Dual Floor Reinforcement with Highest-Severity Evidence
sectorAlerts.headline: Sudan's Amnesty El Fasher crimes-against-humanity report and Yemen's 73-UN-staff detention are the two highest-severity new evidence items this cycle; both entities hold at 0 of 100.
observations: Sudan (0 of 100): the RSF El Obeid offensive threatens 563,000 people in North Kordofan. The Amnesty International July 2026 report documents RSF conduct at El Fasher — the last major North Darfur city not under RSF control — that meets the standard for crimes against humanity. Both items are RSF (non-state) conduct. Sudan's own government conduct collapse is already priced at the floor.

Yemen (0 of 100): 73 UN staff detained by the Houthi de facto authority is one of the largest single-incident UN personnel detentions in the Yemen crisis. 18.3 million people are acutely food insecure. 450-plus health facilities have closed. The Houthi authority is a non-state de facto actor; this conduct is not scored against Yemen's recognized government. The floor reflects Yemen's state-level collapse and the government's own prior conduct.

The structural point: two absolute-floor entities received two of the most severe evidence packages logged this cycle, and neither can move. This is the benchmark's design — extreme actors are anchored at 0 of 100 and the upgrade trigger (implemented ceasefire, aid access, accountability) is the only direction of travel. Monitoring continues for the upgrade trigger; no downgrade is possible.

South Sudan (0 of 100) received a separate floor reinforcement this cycle: 7.8 million food insecure, 73,300 at Phase 5 — a 160% increase since June — and famine risk confirmed in four counties. Three floor-entities in a single cycle is the highest floor-reinforcement count in the July series.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — DRC Ebola Multi-Continent Spread; July Peak Window Active
sectorAlerts.headline: DRC Ebola confirmed in Uganda, France, and Germany in the same assessment window — the first multi-continent spread; DRC holds at 2.3 of 100; July natural peak window active.
observations: The Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak now stands at 1,406 confirmed cases and 438 deaths. Uganda (DRC border), France (imported June 24, confirmed EU first), and Germany (second EU country) represent the outbreak's broadest geographic footprint to date.

The July natural seasonal peak for Bundibugyo Ebola increases the risk that the outbreak outpaces response capacity. No approved Bundibugyo vaccine exists. Contact tracing is at approximately 45 percent. These are response-capacity metrics — the scored element is government conduct toward WHO response corridors.

The floor-crossing trigger — documented deliberate government obstruction of WHO response corridors in Haut-Uele, Ituri, or North Kivu — has not occurred. International spread does not constitute obstruction. DRC government conduct has not included documented WHO-corridor obstruction in any cycle to date.

A DRC Ebola incursion into South Sudan combined with documented failure of South Sudan's own emergency response would be the first cross-floor epidemiological trigger in the series — a new category of scored event that the pipeline is monitoring.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Iran Funeral Window; El Salvador Carry-Forward; China Article 63 Day 5
sectorAlerts.headline: Iran's Khamenei funeral (July 4-9) is procedural; El Salvador's band-crossing proposal carries forward as pending at 20.3 published; China's Article 63 remains unenforced on day 5.
observations: Iran (2.5 of 100): the Khamenei funeral window runs July 4-9. The funeral is a state ceremony — procedural, not a scored harm event. The US-Iran Doha talks are paused. Iran's own conduct — 784-plus executions year-to-date, Strait of Hormuz central channel mine deployment, Gulf strikes — is already priced at 2.5. The protest-suppression watch flag is open: a lethal crackdown during the funeral window would constitute a new scored harm event.

El Salvador (published 20.3 of 100): the July 2 proposal (proposed 15.0 of 100, Critical band) carries forward as pending on July 3. No new evidence was identified that was not already in the July 2 proposal. The duplicate-control rule correctly prevented a second proposal. Published score remains 20.3 of 100.

China (19.5 of 100): the Ethnic Unity Law's Article 63 diaspora liability has been in force for five days without a documented enforcement action. The sub-threshold -1.5 composite dock stands as a forward trigger. The first documented prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member remains the scored event. Five consecutive days without enforcement does not reduce the probability of enforcement — it only confirms that the trigger event has not yet occurred.

Venezuela (18.0 of 100): Day 9 earthquake response. Official toll 2,595. Single-source undercount claim sub-threshold. July 8 checkpoint five days out. This is the fourth consecutive cycle with Venezuela in the boundary-watch set at 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary.

sectorAlerts.sector: Methodology — Record 15 False-Positive Screens; Rotation-State Integrity Corrections
sectorAlerts.headline: Fifteen false-positive screens set a new series record — all correctly disposed of; two rotation-state integrity corrections made this cycle to align drifted composites with published index values.
observations: The 15 false-positive screens are the highest single-cycle count in the benchmark's operational history. The prior record was 14 (July 1, 2026). The breakdown: attribution/other-state (Ukraine, Lebanon), non-state (Sudan, Mali, Burkina Faso, Yemen), exogenous/floor-limitation (Venezuela, South Sudan, Russia), duplicate-control (El Salvador), double-count (China, Nigeria), pre-adjudication (UnitedHealth), and near-floor/procedural (Iran). All seven FP categories were active simultaneously — the first time in the series.
All 15 screens resolved to confirmation without exception. No score was incorrectly moved in either direction. The framework's operational stability under maximum-pressure conditions is validated.

Two rotation-state integrity corrections were made: El Salvador's composite had drifted to 15.x (the proposed score from the pending proposal) — corrected to 20.3 (published). Mali's composite had drifted to 7.5 (an incorrect intermediate value) — corrected to 12.5 (published). Both corrections reflect the core principle: rotation-state tracks the published index, not a proposed or intermediate value.

The Sahel calibration flag (Mali vs. Burkina Faso) is now in its tenth consecutive day. The flag has persisted through the entire June-July series. The absence of a coordinator review timeline is a procedural gap in the pipeline, not an assessment error.

methodologyNotes.name: False-Positive Attribution Framework — 15 Screens (Series Record)
methodologyNotes.description: Fifteen false-positive screens were applied this cycle — a new operational record. The seven active categories: (1) attribution/other-state (Ukraine: Kyiv/toll attributed to Russia; Lebanon: Israeli strikes attributed to Israel); (2) non-state attribution (Sudan: RSF El Obeid/El Fasher; Mali: JNIM Bamako siege; Burkina Faso: JNIM blockades; Yemen: Houthi UN staff detentions); (3) exogenous/floor-limitation (Venezuela: earthquake; South Sudan: Phase 5 famine reinforces floor; Russia: record toll reinforces floor); (4) duplicate-control (El Salvador: same basis as pending July 2 proposal); (5) double-count (China: Ethnic Unity Law already priced; Nigeria: Phase 5 already priced June 19); (6) pre-adjudication (UnitedHealth: DOJ probes not adjudicated); (7) near-floor/procedural (Iran: funeral and paused talks are procedural events). All 15 correctly disposed of under the existing framework without requiring rule modifications.

methodologyNotes.name: Rotation-State Integrity Corrections — Baseline Provenance Protocol
methodologyNotes.description: Two rotation-state integrity corrections were applied this cycle. El Salvador's rotation-state composite had drifted to 15.x — the proposed value from the July 2 pending proposal — rather than the published index value of 20.3. Mali's rotation-state composite had drifted to 7.5 — an incorrect intermediate value — rather than the published index value of 12.5. Both were corrected. The core principle: rotation-state must track the published index (countries.json, fortune-500.json, etc.), not a proposed score or an intermediate entry error. The pending El Salvador proposal (research/change-proposals/el-salvador.json, status: pending) is the operative change record; it does not modify the published index until an apply action is taken.

methodologyNotes.name: Duplicate-Control Rule (El Salvador) — No Second Proposal Emitted
methodologyNotes.description: The July 3 El Salvador assessment encountered the same evidence basis (indefinite re-election amendment, Cristosal exile, 86 political prisoners, child life-sentences) already captured in the July 2 pending downgrade proposal. The duplicate-control rule was applied: when a pending proposal covers the same evidence basis and no materially new evidence has emerged, no second proposal is emitted. The pending proposal stands as the operative change record. The baseline used in the July 3 assessment is the published value (20.3), not the proposed value (15.0). This distinction is critical: the baseline for subsequent assessments is always the published score until a proposal is applied.

forwardTriggers.trigger: 14-day reassessment checkpoint — independently corroborated suppression or falsification of casualty data, or documented deliberate aid obstruction, is the downgrade trigger from 18.0 of 100. Sustained effective transparent relief is the upgrade trigger. Action (ACT) and Accountability (ACC) watch flags open. Official toll 2,595; Day 9.

Close of Khamenei state funeral window — a documented lethal crackdown on mourners or demonstrators, or mass arrest of political figures, during the July 4-9 funeral window constitutes a new scored harm event from the near-floor 2.5 of 100. Funeral without lethal suppression continues the already-priced pattern.
Full resolution-phase reassessment due. Conversion trigger: reversal of the June 8 codified-impunity law and release of detained union leaders.

Q2 2026 earnings call — material DOJ disclosures, investor-facing admissions about probe scope, indictment, or settlement announcements are scored triggers under Accountability (ACC) and Integrity (INT) dimensions.

July natural Bundibugyo Ebola peak window active. 1,406 cases and 438 deaths as of July 3. Uganda, France, and Germany cases confirmed. Documented government obstruction of WHO response corridors is the floor-crossing trigger toward 0 of 100.
EU AI Act full applicability — general-purpose AI transparency obligations, Article 50 labeling, and whistleblower protections activate. No public compliance roadmap from xAI.
EU AI Act full applicability — same obligations apply. 42-state attorney general proceedings ongoing.
EU AI Act full applicability — 30 days away. Anthropic's compliance infrastructure is documented. White House 30-day vetting framework resolution is the next forward factor.
August IPC lean-season update — IPC Phase 5 Borno trajectory and actual WFP food-assistance cuts are scored triggers from the June 19 band crossing to 18.0 of 100.

Famine declaration window open through September 2026. Buur Hakaba and Baidoa at IPC AMN Phase 5. A formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration combined with documented government aid obstruction is a scored change trigger.

2027 election under the July 2025 indefinite re-election constitutional amendment. Enforcement of the amendment in the 2027 election is the next structured forward trigger. A further HRD imprisonment or mass-detention escalation constitutes a deeper downgrade trigger from the proposed Critical band. Due-process restoration and civil-society reinstatement are the upgrade triggers.

First documented Article 63 enforcement action under the Ethnic Unity and Progress Law — prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member. Law entered force July 1; five days without enforcement as of July 3. Any first enforcement constitutes new realized harm scorable toward Equity and Boundaries docks.

Russia at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. Cessation of deliberate attacks on civilians, return of forcibly deported children, and cooperation with the EU Special Tribunal are the upgrade-trigger conditions. No lower score is possible.

An implemented civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed humanitarian access is the scored positive own-conduct trigger. El Fasher's status as the last major North Darfur city not under RSF control is the sentinel geography for the next major observable event.
A new ceasefire AND Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control of southern Lebanon AND reconstruction delivery are the scored positive-conduct triggers from 17.2 of 100.

A realized Ebola incursion from DRC's Haut-Uele province combined with documented failure of South Sudan's own emergency response would constitute a scorable own-conduct event — the first cross-floor epidemiological trigger in the series.

Coordinator-level Sahel-band calibration review — Mali (12.5 of 100) versus Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100). Tenth consecutive day without a timeline. No assessed one-off possible until coordinator review establishes the calibrated range.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: Russia's July 2026 civilian toll (286 killed, 1,388 injured in three days) is producing the fastest accumulation rate in the EU Special Tribunal's active evidentiary window. Does the Tribunal's activation constitute a new scored Accountability event for Russia, or does it remain a forward-trigger pending a formal judgment?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: Russia holds at 0 of 100 — the floor. The EU Special Tribunal is active. Does tribunal activation score under Accountability before a judgment, or only after?
